emissions.methodology: GHG Protocol, ISAE 3000 assured by Intertek
emissions.cdp_notes: CDP Climate: A- (2023), Supplier Engagement: A (2024)

net_zero.notes: Net-zero by 2050 or earlier; already near-zero operational emissions

sbti.notes: REMOVED from SBTi in May 2023 due to parent group (Adani) involvement in coal. Despite pure-play RE operations, SBTi Fossil Fuel Policy triggered removal.
sbti.removal_reason: SBTi Fossil Fuel Policy - parent group involvement in coal

coal_policy.no_new_coal_note: Pure-play RE company - no thermal assets

capex_allocation.clean_capex_definition: All CAPEX is for RE projects
capex_allocation.notes: INR 2 lakh crore (USD 24 billion) committed for 50 GW buildout

coal_plants.notes: Pure-play RE company - no coal or thermal assets

debt_to_ebitda.notes: Using gross debt 80,040 Cr / EBITDA 10,205 Cr. Net Debt/EBITDA 6.3x also above threshold.

interest_coverage.notes: EBITDA 10,205 Cr / Interest 5,492 Cr = 1.86x. EBIT-based: 1.40x. Below 2.0x comfort threshold typical for infrastructure.

ffo_to_debt_pct.notes: OCF 8,364 Cr / Total Debt 80,040 Cr = 10.45%. Rating agencies use FFO ~3,942 Cr (post-tax) yielding ~4.9%.

current_ratio.notes: Current Assets 8,622 Cr / Current Liabilities 16,888 Cr. Reliance on rolling debt facilities typical for high-capex infrastructure.

debt_to_capital_pct.notes: High leverage (D/E 6.6x) typical of infrastructure buildout phase. Equity growing as profits accumulate.

credit_metrics_b5.notes: ALL 5 METRICS FAIL. Despite strong EBITDA margin (91.7%) and investment-grade ratings, leverage metrics are stretched: Debt/EBITDA 7.84x, Interest Coverage 1.86x, FFO/Debt 10.45%, Current Ratio 0.51x, Debt/Capital 86.8%. This reflects aggressive 50 GW buildout financing - typical for high-growth RE infrastructure. Credit ratings (CRISIL AA+, Fitch BBB-) supported by 25-year PPA revenue visibility.
credit_metrics_b5.source: Calculated from AGEL Audited Consolidated Financial Statements FY2024-25

verification_checklist.sbti_verification_note: Removed from SBTi May 2023 due to parent group coal involvement
verification_checklist.b5_data_note: All balance sheet data resolved 2026-01-14 from audited FY25 statements

analyst_notes: Adani Green Energy Limited (AGEL) is India's largest renewable energy company and a pure-play RE utility with 17.2 GW of solar/wind capacity (100% RE). Despite exemplary environmental credentials (CDP A-, net-zero 2050, 99.8% lower emissions than grid), the company faces significant financial stress. With complete FY2024-25 audited data, ALL 5 B5 METRICS FAIL: Debt/EBITDA 7.84x (threshold <4.0x), Interest Coverage 1.86x (threshold >2.5x), FFO/Debt 10.45% (threshold >20%), Current Ratio 0.51x (threshold >1.0x), Debt/Capital 86.8% (threshold <60%). This reflects aggressive 50 GW buildout financing - typical for high-growth RE infrastructure. Credit ratings (CRISIL AA+, Fitch BBB-) remain investment-grade due to 25-year PPA revenue visibility and 91.7% EBITDA margins. SBTi validation was REMOVED in May 2023 because the parent Adani Group has significant coal exposure through Adani Power - this is a governance/boundary issue, not an operational one. B4 PASSES via net-zero 2050 + 100% RE pathway. ETCB outcomes: B1 PASS (0% coal), B2 PASS (minimal emissions), B3 PASS (no thermal), B4 PASS (net-zero + RE share), B5 FAIL (0/5 metrics). Final score: 4/5 Medium-high. Interesting contrast: Coal-heavy sibling Adani Power (IN003) scores 3/5 with perfect B5 (5/5 pass), while 100% RE AGEL scores 4/5 but fails B5 entirely due to growth financing.
